Alrighty, so I'm going to unpack what a full three SPP stack looks like. This is the full workhorse version and I'm not going to re-go through the tags that I've already been through on the first video. So we're just gonna, I'm gonna go from here. So is this custom field blank? If it is, it sends them down, down this path to receive the first SPP in the stack. Over here we're asking, does SPP-AUE 1 completed exist? So if it's no, it's asking that question. If they've only been down this one, that is going to be true. So if I click on that, you can see does that match that. In the case that somebody's been down here the first time and only once, that will be true. So yes, and all it does is just like exactly the same as this side here, it sends them down this path, which then we have it writes SOE 2 completed. And so let's go back up here again. So does that, is that a match? So let's say that they've got two now. Is it blank? No, they go down here. Do they have that, which is number one? The answer will be no at this point because they've already received it, which means they'll receive this one. So this is, do they have number two? The answer is yes, because I've just been, it's been, it's been marked over here, further down. So this will be true. And then it just sends them down number three. So this is how we get three in a stack by using these three decisions. If no, which means they've received three. So the column here, they've received completed three at this point, which will be no to this one. So no, sends them down the last decision tree. And if you hear, we have the VIP, have they received three? Yes, they have received three, which means they've received all, all SPPs in the stack. Well, now we want them to receive the VIP email. So it'll be yes to that. And they will receive that. And then it'll mark them as completed the VIP and the automation ends. Yeah, that's pretty simple. It looks complicated, but the way it works is pretty easy.
